text.title: Vision Agentic Pipeline — Production Architecture Core Rules

rule.statement: Vision pipeline MUST split work into three subagents: router, extractor, validator.
rule.rationale: Single-prompt designs regress on accuracy as prompt size grows; separation lets each agent be tuned independently.

rule.statement: Extractor MUST return a Pydantic model; raw text returns are rejected.
rule.rationale: Structured output prevents downstream string-parsing fragility.

rule.statement: Validator MUST be able to veto extractor output and route to human review.
rule.rationale: Without veto, garbage extractions silently propagate to downstream systems.

rule.statement: Router MUST run a cheaper model than the extractor (e.g. haiku for router, sonnet for extractor).
rule.rationale: Routing is a classification; spending an opus call on a 2-class decision is a waste.

rule.statement: Extractor MUST retry once on schema-validation failure with a 'fix the JSON' system message.
rule.rationale: VLMs intermittently emit malformed JSON; one retry recovers ~80% of failures.

rule.statement: Pipeline MUST key its result cache by SHA-256 of the image bytes + doc_type.
rule.rationale: Re-running the pipeline on the same image is common; idempotency saves VLM credits.
